records.ety: [AS. is; akin to G. & Goth. ist, L. est, Gr. <?/, Skr. asti. √9. Cf. Am, Entity, Essence, Absent.]

defs: The third person singular of the substantive verb be, in the indicative mood, present tense; as, he is; he is a man. See Be.
quotes: For thy is I come, and eke Alain.
notes: ☞ In some varieties of the Northern dialect of Old English, is was used for all persons of the singular.
☞ The idiom of using the present for future events sure to happen is a relic of Old English in which the present and future had the same form; as, this year Christmas is on Friday.

defs: A prefix or combining form, indicating identity, or equality; the same numerical value; as in isopod, isomorphous, isochromatic.
Applied to certain compounds having the same composition but different properties; as in isocyanic.

Applied to compounds of certain isomeric series in whose structure one carbon atom, at least, is connected with three other carbon atoms; -- contrasted with neo- and normal; as in isoparaffine; isopentane.
